title: entada glandulosa
intro: Entada glandulosa, also known as the fish-poison tree or fish-killer tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Entada glandulosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
